skill_commit_note: SKILL.md and references/* were stable during the sweep. evals.json received in-flight substring relaxations (commits 178f678, 0cb131d, 8b3fa1b) — the relaxations are documented in SUMMARY.md ('Substring relaxations applied'). For 'what skill version was being measured' interpret as the 874516a state of skills/spyglass/SKILL.md and references/.
spyglass_src_note: Not recorded at dispatch time — see Round-D dispatch templates (skills/spyglass/evals/dispatch_prompts.md) which now require pinning. The sweep's eval agents had access to a local Spyglass checkout at the path above but the commit hash was not captured. Treat field-level claims grounded in this source as 'verified at sweep time, re-verify before pinning'.
model_note: Eval-runner used the parent-orchestrator's model. Subagent dispatches inherited the same model via the Task tool default.
dispatch_prompt_template: Inline (per-batch, typed by orchestrator). Templates extracted post-hoc to skills/spyglass/evals/dispatch_prompts.md.
dispatch_prompt_contamination_note: Round-C without_skill prompts did not explicitly forbid reading skills/spyglass/. 5/133 baseline runs touched the skill bundle (eval-058 read ripple_pipeline.md; eval-034 grepped references; eval-042/066/082 listed dirs). Bias direction: against the skill (baselines got partial skill-content access). Round-D templates fix this.

artifacts.analysis: summary/ (12 plots, SUMMARY.md, category_breakdown.csv, ref_utilization.json, script_utilization.json)
